description_long: This is a classic stovetop moka pot, commonly used for brewing espresso-style coffee. The body is octagonal and made of a dark, matte-finished metal, likely aluminum, with visible wear and subtle marbling. It features a prominent spout for pouring, a sturdy side-mounted handle made of black plastic, and a small, round knob on top of the lid for easy opening. The lid appears to be hinged and fits snugly over the upper chamber. The base is also octagonal and slightly wider, with a safety valve visible on one side, typical of moka pots for pressure release. The overall design is utilitarian and plain, with no visible decorative patterns.
description: A dark, octagonal metal moka pot with a black side handle, spout, and a hinged lid with a knob. The surface is matte and plain, showing some wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout, octagonal body, and the top knob clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the black, side-mounted handle and the spout's profile.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the handle's full shape and the hinge of the lid.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle and the plain, faceted body.
